title: asplenium scolopendrium subsp. antri-jovis
intro: Asplenium scolopendrium subsp. antri-jovis, also known as Hart's Tongue fern, is a subspecies of the Asplenium scolopendrium fern, characterized by its distinctive fronds resembling a tongue. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Hart's Tongue fern include its potential to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and processing the fronds for use in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal remedy.
